mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 6% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.5 million units signals a strengthening demand for residential construction that boosts economic growth and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 99 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and economic growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 2% signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods that boosts economic growth and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising real disposable personal income are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in initial unemployment claims above 1.5 million signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising initial unemployment claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the consumer price index above 3% signals a strengthening monetary policy that reduces inflation expectations and boosts corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising consumer price index are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3% signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts economic growth and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
